definition: To change your behavior or ideas to fit a new situation.
example: Good teachers adapt their lessons to the needs of each class.

definition: A state in which different things exist in equal or correct amounts.
example: Students need a balance between study time and rest.

definition: To record or express something clearly.
example: That sentence captures the feeling of the story perfectly.

definition: To make an idea or statement easier to understand.
example: The teacher used another example to clarify the grammar point.

definition: To join together or create a relationship between ideas.
example: Reading helps learners connect new words with familiar topics.

definition: To think carefully about something before deciding.
example: Consider the context before you choose the best translation.

definition: Having the ability to produce original ideas.
example: A creative activity can make vocabulary practice more memorable.

definition: A discussion in which people express different opinions.
example: The class held a short debate using the new vocabulary words.

definition: A feeling of great pleasure or joy.
example: The students reacted with delight when they understood the joke.

definition: Working well without wasting time or effort.
example: Flashcards are an efficient tool for reviewing vocabulary.

definition: To give support or confidence to someone.
example: Positive feedback can encourage learners to keep practicing.

definition: To learn about something by examining it carefully.
example: Students can explore new meanings through context clues.

definition: Special attention given to one thing.
example: Today's lesson has a strong focus on travel vocabulary.
Before the activity, gather the words you want to review.
An honest self-check helps students improve faster.

definition: To become better or make something better.
example: Daily review can improve vocabulary retention over time.

definition: To watch carefully and notice details.
example: Observe how the word changes meaning in a new sentence.
Take a moment to reflect on the words you missed today.
